role: 1st President of the Islamic Republic of Iran (1980–1981)
bio: Abolhassan Banisadr was the first president of the Islamic Republic, elected in January 1980 with 76% of the vote. An economist and political thinker who had been close to Khomeini in exile, he quickly came into conflict with the clerical establishment and the Islamic Republican Party. He was impeached by parliament in June 1981 and fled to France, where he lived in exile until his death in 2021.
editorial_notes: Died 9 October 2021 in Paris. Impeached June 22, 1981.
